name: Introduction to GIS for Environmental Science
description: Foundation course covering core GIS concepts including coordinate reference systems map projections spatial data models and basic vector and raster analysis. Students work with QGIS and explore real-world environmental datasets.

name: Spatial Analysis with QGIS and R
description: Intermediate course covering vector overlay operations raster analysis geostatistics and spatial statistics using QGIS and the R sf and terra packages. Students apply techniques to marine and terrestrial conservation datasets.

name: Remote Sensing Fundamentals
description: Survey of passive and active remote sensing platforms including multispectral Landsat imagery Sentinel-2 and drone-based surveys. Topics include image classification spectral indices and change detection for habitat monitoring.

name: Cartographic Design and Map Communication (Fall 2025)
description: Completed prerequisite module covering cartographic principles typography color theory and effective visual communication for scientific maps. Grounding in print and web map design using QGIS and Adobe Illustrator.

name: GIS Capstone: Coral Reef Spatial Data
description: Shared workspace for the GIS certification capstone practicum. Students develop an end-to-end spatial analysis project using real-world reef site data including georeferencing photo-transect waypoints habitat classification and reef-site map production.

name: GIS Certification Program Orientation 2026
descriptionHeading: Welcome to the 2026 GIS Certification Cohort
description: Orientation workspace for the 2026 cohort of the Pacific Environmental GIS Certificate program. Contains cohort schedule software setup guides QGIS and R installation tutorials and program policies.

name: GIS Certification Program Orientation 2025 (Archived)
description: Prior-year orientation workspace retained for reference. Contains 2025 cohort schedule and initial software setup guides.
